old_value:
{"id":"project-LICHEN-x7iu","title":"ReassemblyManager.receive may evict receiver mid-datagram on overflow","description":"File: python/src/lichen/schc/reassembly.py:210-212

ReassemblyManager.receive() creates a new receiver for unknown keys, then evicts the oldest if capacity is exceeded:
  receiver = FragmentReceiver(self.window_size)
  self._contexts[key] = receiver
  while len(self._contexts) > self.max_contexts:
      self._contexts.popitem(last=False)  # evict oldest

This eviction happens BEFORE the new fragment is processed. If a new sender appears when max_contexts receivers exist, the oldest in-progress datagram is silently discarded.

While this is documented behavior (bounded memory), there's no indication to the caller that a datagram was aborted. The evicted receiver's sender would eventually timeout waiting for ACKs.

More critically, the eviction does not send a NACK to the evicted sender, so the sender has no immediate feedback that its datagram was dropped.

Consider:
1. Returning an indication of eviction in ReceiverResult
2. Logging or metrics hook for monitoring
3. Optional behavior to reject new senders instead of evicting

Category: PROTOCOL","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","owner":"me@mark.atwood.name","created_at":"2026-07-12T06:29:08.043801Z","created_by":"Mark Atwood","updated_at":"2026-07-12T06:29:08.043835Z"}

{"id":"project-LICHEN-x7iu","title":"ReassemblyManager.receive may evict receiver mid-datagram on overflow","description":"File: python/src/lichen/schc/reassembly.py:210-212

ReassemblyManager.receive() creates a new receiver for unknown keys, then evicts the oldest if capacity is exceeded:
  receiver = FragmentReceiver(self.window_size)
  self._contexts[key] = receiver
  while len(self._contexts) > self.max_contexts:
      self._contexts.popitem(last=False)  # evict oldest

This eviction happens BEFORE the new fragment is processed. If a new sender appears when max_contexts receivers exist, the oldest in-progress datagram is silently discarded.

While this is documented behavior (bounded memory), there's no indication to the caller that a datagram was aborted. The evicted receiver's sender would eventually timeout waiting for ACKs.

More critically, the eviction does not send a NACK to the evicted sender, so the sender has no immediate feedback that its datagram was dropped.

Consider:
1. Returning an indication of eviction in ReceiverResult
2. Logging or metrics hook for monitoring
3. Optional behavior to reject new senders instead of evicting

new_value: Added evicted: bool=False to ReceiverResult; set in ReassemblyManager.receive() when eviction occurs. Provides indication without breaking existing tests or API. Ruff clean. Fixes x7iu.
